domain_1_personality.domain_summary: Most shaped by emotional_stability and extraversion.

top_contributors.display_name: Feelings of Uselessness or Low Utility

domain_2_self_esteem.domain_summary: Self-esteem (normal) was most influenced by perceived competence relative to peers and sense of self-pride.

domain_3_mood_sleep.domain_summary: Moderate Depression mood signal, driven mainly by symptom burden summation and calculated sleep duration.

top_contributors.display_name: Fear of Missing Out / Digital Dependence
Neglect of Domestic Duties for Internet

domain_4_digital_and_social.domain_summary: Loneliness and digital-use signals were most influenced by fear of missing out / digital dependence and defensive secretiveness regarding usage.

domain_5_occupational_burnout.domain_summary: High Burnout Risk, driven mainly by general perceived stress load and daily meeting & communication load.

domain_6_severe_clinical.domain_summary: Mild Symptomatic Profile, most associated with autonomic hyperarousal & guardedness and maladaptive rumination cycle. An atypical response pattern was also flagged for review.

global_synthesis: COMPLEX PROFILE EVALUATION: Unsupervised screening models flag an atypical response configuration. While tracking markers align closely with the Mild Symptomatic Profile, the overall structure falls outside standard baseline distributions concurrently with elevated clinical metrics (PHQ-9 Sum: 12). A comprehensive professional differential evaluation is recommended to reconcile these mixed indicators.

plain_language_summary.opening: This assessment surfaced some signals worth taking seriously, particularly on the clinical screening section, where responses point toward a mild symptomatic profile.
plain_language_summary.domain_recap: Across the six areas measured, their emotional stability stands out as notably higher than their other traits; self-esteem scored 20 out of 40 (normal); a PHQ-9 mood screening score of 12 (moderate depression), with about 17.3 hours of sleep; moderate loneliness signals alongside an internet-use pattern score of 27; and a high burnout risk (index 6.6) in their work life.
plain_language_summary.closing: Given these clinical screening results, we'd strongly encourage speaking with a mental health professional -- a doctor, therapist, or counselor -- for a proper evaluation. This summary is based on self-reported questionnaire patterns and is not a diagnosis.
plain_language_summary.full_text: This assessment surfaced some signals worth taking seriously, particularly on the clinical screening section, where responses point toward a mild symptomatic profile. Across the six areas measured, their emotional stability stands out as notably higher than their other traits; self-esteem scored 20 out of 40 (normal); a PHQ-9 mood screening score of 12 (moderate depression), with about 17.3 hours of sleep; moderate loneliness signals alongside an internet-use pattern score of 27; and a high burnout risk (index 6.6) in their work life. Given these clinical screening results, we'd strongly encourage speaking with a mental health professional -- a doctor, therapist, or counselor -- for a proper evaluation. This summary is based on self-reported questionnaire patterns and is not a diagnosis.
